value.market_read: Headline: A 6.1 mb crude draw to 412.1 mb dominated the week, but the real story sits at Cushing, where stocks fell another 1.1 mb to 18.96 mb — now firmly below the ~25 mb operational floor. Refinery utilization eased 0.6 pts to 96.1% with crude inputs at 17.11 mb/d, keeping the demand pull on the system strong heading into peak summer. Driver: The crude draw is the product of high run rates and steady export pull, with 4-week crude exports at 4.93 mb/d against 5.75 mb/d imports. Products diverged: gasoline built 2.1 mb and distillate built 3.1 mb. The distillate build came on soft 3.63 mb/d demand failing to absorb steady output — an expected outcome, not a surprise. Gasoline demand softened to 8.83 mb/d (4-wk) from 8.9 mb/d prior, taking some shine off the build. Trade flows: Crude imports ticked up to 5.75 mb/d (4-wk) from 5.65 mb/d while exports slipped to 4.93 from 5.07, a mild net loosening that nonetheless could not offset the Cushing drain. PADD III crude drew a heavy 4.0 mb as Gulf refiners ran at 96.7% and exports continued to clear barrels. Watch next week: Cushing deliverability is the single most important variable — at 18.96 mb and drawing ~1 mb/wk for five straight weeks, tank minimums are the binding constraint and prompt WTI backwardation should stay firm. Watch whether the draw rate moderates or extends into the low-18s, and track hurricane-season risk now that the June 1 Atlantic window is open.

padd.padd1: Gasoline built 1.21 mb into the summer driving season as East Coast refinery utilization collapsed 10.6 pts to 76.5%, pointing to a turnaround or unplanned outage that will lean on waterborne and Colonial supply. Distillate drew 2.12 mb and jet built 1.32 mb, a mixed barrel slate. With local runs gutted, PADD I leans heavily on Colonial nominations to keep gasoline stocked. Watch: NYH RBOB cash structure — if the prompt diff holds a premium (backwardation), barrels get pulled north on Colonial and the build accelerates; a slip into contango would stall PADD I supply with runs already this weak.
padd.padd2: Cushing fell another 1.08 mb to 18.96 mb — already well below the ~25 mb operational floor and now down a cumulative 4 mb over five weeks from 23.02 mb. Sub-floor tank levels are a deliverability problem, and prompt WTI backwardation should stay supported as marketers compete for scarce wet barrels. Regional crude drew 0.53 mb while PADD II refiners ran flat-out at 100.0% utilization, down just 1.2 pts. Distillate built 0.88 mb. Watch: Whether the ~1 mb/wk Cushing draw rate persists into the low-18s — at these levels tank minimums, not economics, set the floor and any further drain tightens deliverability stress.
padd.padd3: Gulf Coast crude drew a heavy 3.98 mb as refiners pushed utilization up 0.5 pts to 96.7% near peak and export loadings continued to clear barrels. Distillate built 2.96 mb on the high run rates while gasoline added 0.83 mb and jet drew 0.79 mb. PADD III remains the system's pressure valve, feeding both export docks and the Cushing-starved midcontinent. Watch: Hurricane-season risk now that the June 1 Atlantic window is open — with runs this close to ceiling, any Gulf storm landfall would strand barrels and ricochet straight into already sub-floor Cushing deliverability.
padd.padd4: Rocky Mountain crude drew 0.53 mb as utilization eased 1.8 pts to 96.6%, a modest pullback in a small but turnaround-sensitive region. Product moves were negligible — gasoline flat at +0.02 mb, distillate +0.07 mb, jet +0.03 mb — consistent with steady local demand and no refinery disruption. Bakken and DJ-basin supply remains the swing input here. Watch: Any unplanned turnaround that would amplify the crude draw, given how thin regional inventory cover runs in PADD IV.
padd.padd5: West Coast crude drew 1.64 mb while refinery utilization edged up 0.8 pts to 94.2%, supporting the build across the product slate. Gasoline added 0.91 mb and distillate built 1.27 mb in the CARB-isolated market, while jet was flat at +0.07 mb. LA Basin margins remain the key driver given the region's structural import reliance and the Rodeo conversion's reshaping of in-basin balances. Watch: CARB gasoline differentials into peak summer driving demand — any refinery hiccup tightens this islanded market fast with limited outside relief.
